claims.claim: dialog-edit-mcp.tsx follows same pattern as dialog-edit-agent.tsx
evidence: Both use `useDialog()` from @opencode-ai/ui/context/dialog
Both use `createMemo` for isEditing derived state
Both use `createSignal` for form fields initialized from `props.entry`
Both have `handleSave` that constructs the entry and calls `dialog.close()`
Both render a `<Dialog>` with `class="w-full max-w-[480px] mx-auto"`
Both use `Button variant="ghost"` for cancel and `Button variant="primary"` for save
Both disable save button when name is empty
observations: DialogEditMcp uses hardcoded English labels ("Name", "Type", "Command", "Timeout (seconds, optional)", "URL") while DialogEditAgent uses i18n keys throughout
DialogEditMcp title for create mode is hardcoded "Add MCP Server" instead of using language.t("dialog.mcp.addTitle")
DialogEditMcp doesn't have a `description` prop or a description field unlike dialog-edit-agent's Select for model

claims.claim: IPC handlers use electron-store correctly (same pattern as desktop-custom-agents)
evidence: RESERVED_STORE_NAMES includes 'desktop-mcp-servers' — blocks generic store-get/set/delete/clear access
getStore('desktop-mcp-servers') mirrors getStore('desktop-custom-agents') pattern exactly
store.set('servers', ...) uses key 'servers' — consistent with get-desktop-custom-agents using store.get('agents')
validateAndFilterMcpServers wraps raw data identically to validateAndFilterAgents
Both use ipcMain.handle with same return pattern

claims.claim: Delete flow handles partial failures gracefully (disconnect → auth.remove → electron-store)
evidence: confirmRemove wraps each step in try/catch
First step: sdk.client.mcp.disconnect — warns on failure
Second step: sdk.client.mcp.auth.remove — catches silently (comment says method may not exist)
Third step: electron-store removal via api.getMcpServers/filter/setMcpServers — warns on failure
confirmDelete signal gates the confirmation UI, cleared on completion or cancel
observations: auth.remove empty catch swallows potential 'method not found' errors, which is intentional but could hide legitimate errors
If api is undefined (non-desktop context), store removal is silently skipped — graceful degradation

claims.claim: Existing functionality is preserved (health indicators, toggle, auth trigger, inline add form)
evidence: McpHealthIndicator component added as a pure additive function, doesn't affect existing code paths
The enable/disable toggle still uses useMutation with sdk.client.mcp.connect/disconnect/authenticate
The inline add form still works via store.mode === 'add' flow
The select/click handler still calls toggle.mutate(i.name) for toggling
The inline search/list/filter/handleKeyDown pattern is preserved from original implementation

claims.claim: Edge cases handled: empty name, missing command/URL, timeout validation
evidence: Save button disabled when: !name() || (type() === 'local' ? !command() : !url()) — prevents saving with empty name/command/url
timeoutValue uses Number.parseInt with radix 10
command string is split on whitespace and filtered for empty segments: split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean)
observations: Timeout parseInt('', 10) returns NaN — but empty string is falsy, so the spread {...(timeoutValue() ? { timeout: ... } : {})} skips it; passes correctly when empty

BUT: if user enters 'abc' in the number field, timeoutValue() is 'abc' (truthy), parseInt('abc', 10) returns NaN, and { timeout: NaN } is spread into the config — this is a bug. Should use Number.isNaN guard.
No validation on timeout range (accepts any number, even negative)
Edit dialog's disabled field for name prevents renaming — this is by design but means users can't fix a typo in server name

claims.claim: Editing onSave just closes dialog (no persistence yet — follow-up)
evidence: onSave callback in dialog-select-mcp.tsx: `(entry) => { setEditingEntry(undefined) }` — only clears the editing signal
handleSave in dialog-edit-mcp.tsx calls `props.onSave(entry)` then `dialog.close()`
No IPC calls to set-desktop-mcp-servers are made from the edit flow
DropdownMenu.Item with onSelect → handleEdit explicitly present
Uses language.t('dialog.mcp.action.edit') for label
observations: BUG: onSelect handler passes stub config: `handleEdit({ name: i.name, config: { type: 'local', command: [], enabled: true } })` — the config does NOT come from the actual server data. The sync data only provides status (connected/failed/etc.), not the stored config. Results in editing dialog always showing local type with empty command regardless of actual server configuration.
Delete menu item uses i18n key 'dialog.server.menu.delete' which is shared with server-side menu, not MCP-specific

claims.claim: McpRemoteConfig type from SDK is compatible with app types
evidence: SDK McpRemoteConfig { type: 'remote', url: string, enabled?: boolean, headers?: {}, oauth?: McpOAuthConfig | false, timeout?: number }
App's McpLocalConfig { type: 'local', command: string[], environment?: Record<string, string>, timeout?: number, enabled?: boolean } — shapes are compatible
TypeScript typecheck passes with 0 errors confirming type compatibility

claims.claim: Validation helpers in IPC protect against corrupt data
evidence: isValidMcpEntry checks: val is object, name is string, config is object, config.type is 'local' with Array.isArray(command), or config.type is 'remote' with typeof url === 'string'
validateAndFilterMcpServers wraps isValidMcpEntry, filters invalid entries, warns via console.warn
Returns empty array for non-array input
observations: Validation does not check for missing `enabled` field — this is acceptable since it's optional
No check for non-empty command array (empty array [] would pass validation) — acceptable for edge cases

claims.claim: ElectronAPI types are consistent across the stack
evidence: preload/types.ts: getMcpServers: () => Promise<unknown[]>, setMcpServers: (servers: unknown[]) => Promise<void>
preload/index.ts: getMcpServers: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('get-desktop-mcp-servers'), setMcpServers: (servers) => ipcRenderer.invoke('set-desktop-mcp-servers', servers)
ipc.ts: handlers registered as 'get-desktop-mcp-servers' and 'set-desktop-mcp-servers'
app/src/app.tsx: Window.api.getMcpServers and setMcpServers as optional methods returning Promise<unknown[]>

claims.claim: Nested Dialog rendering is structurally sound
evidence: DialogEditMcp is rendered inside a `<Show when={editingEntry()}>` block inside the parent Dialog's content area
DialogEditMcp itself contains a `<Dialog>` component with its own `useDialog()` context
observations: Rendering a Dialog inside another Dialog creates nested dialog content. The UI component library may handle this via portals, but DOM structure will have nested dialog elements. If the library doesn't support nested dialogs, this could cause z-index or focus-trap issues.
The alternative pattern (used by dialog-edit-agent elsewhere) renders edit dialogs at the top level via portals or conditional rendering outside the parent dialog's content area

action_items.description: Edit button passes stub config instead of actual server config — editing form always shows default values regardless of actual server. Fix: retrieve stored config from electron-store or SDK and pass actual McpServerEntry to handleEdit.

Timeout field: Number.parseInt can produce NaN when user enters non-numeric text. Add isNaN guard: `...(timeoutValue() ? { timeout: Number.parseInt(timeoutValue(), 10) } : {})` should check `!Number.isNaN(parsed)`.
DialogEditMcp uses hardcoded English labels instead of i18n keys. Use language.t('dialog.mcp.form.name'), etc.
"Add MCP Server" title is hardcoded instead of using language.t('dialog.mcp.addTitle') or language.t('dialog.mcp.action.add')
Nested Dialog component — consider rendering DialogEditMcp outside the parent Dialog content area to avoid potential z-index/focus-trap issues.

deferred_seams: Editing persistence — onSave only closes dialog, follow-up needed for set-desktop-mcp-servers IPC call
Environment variable editing in MCP local config form
OAuth configuration in MCP remote config form
Header configuration in MCP remote config form
